source.title: Dopamine release from the locus coeruleus to the dorsal hippocampus promotes spatial learning and memory
source.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

confidence_rationale: Rigorous optogenetic + neurochemical study establishing LC as the primary source of dopamine in dorsal hippocampus. The finding directly complicates MECH-075's framing of BG/VTA as the dopamine gain source for hippocampal attractors: for dorsal HPC (spatial/planning navigation), the relevant catecholaminergic input is from LC, not VTA. Evidence is mixed — it supports dopaminergic/catecholaminergic gain modulation in HPC but challenges the VTA-centric framing of MECH-075.
failure_signatures: VTA dopaminergic axons are sparse in dorsal hippocampus — the premise that VTA/BG dopamine directly modulates dorsal HPC attractor dynamics is contradicted by this anatomical finding.
The functional role (spatial learning and memory enhancement) is shown for LC-mediated dopamine but the attractor-depth mechanism (MECH-075's core claim) is not directly tested.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Photostimulation of locus coeruleus axons in dorsal hippocampus releases dopamine and enhances spatial object recognition and memory. VTA dopamine axons are sparse in dorsal hippocampus. LC neurons co-release dopamine and norepinephrine, with dopamine release from LC axons being the functional signal for dorsal HPC memory enhancement.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-075 claims BG dopaminergic signals set gain/threshold on hippocampal attractor basins, modulating trajectory retrieval probability. Kempadoo et al. establish that for dorsal hippocampus (the spatial/planning substrate of E3's viability map), the dopaminergic gain input comes primarily from locus coeruleus, not VTA/BG. This requires a reframing of MECH-075: the 'gain' control on dorsal HPC attractors is LC-mediated (via co-released DA/NE) and responds to arousal/novelty signals routed through LC, not solely BG-mediated reinforcement signals routed through VTA. VTA dopamine may set gain in ventral HPC (motivation/affect), while LC sets gain in dorsal HPC (spatial/planning precision).
mapping.mapping_caveat: MECH-075 may need subdivision: dorsal HPC attractor gain = LC-mediated; ventral HPC attractor gain = VTA-mediated. Kempadoo only studies dorsal HPC; whether VTA does modulate ventral HPC (as Lisman & Grace 2005 implies) is addressed separately. The attractor-depth gain mechanism is inferred from spatial learning enhancement, not directly from attractor dynamics measurement.
mapping.source_context: Optogenetic activation of LC axons in dorsal hippocampus of mice; HPLC neurochemistry for dopamine quantification; novel object location and recognition behavioral tasks.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (Kandel lab, PNAS, rigorous optogenetics). Mapping fidelity moderate-low — the paper challenges the MECH-075 VTA framing rather than directly supporting it; supports the broader catecholaminergic gain modulation principle but with different anatomy than assumed. Transfer risk moderate: mouse study, human HPC catecholaminergic innervation has same LC-dominant pattern in dorsal HPC.
